aliases: familial fibrous dysplasia of the jaws
symptom: cheeks that slowly rounded out on both sides over a couple of years
hook: both sides of the lower jaw were ballooned out by matching bubbly cysts
whatYouSee: Both sides of the jaw are expanded by symmetrical bubbly cysts.
whyItMatters: A genetic condition replaces the jawbone with fibrous cystic tissue in childhood.
treatment: Often it just needs watching because it tends to shrink on its own after puberty and surgery is saved for the severe cases.
takeaway: The jaw often quietly returns to normal after puberty.
laypersonQuestion: Does this jaw look the same on both sides?
seedHint: Look left then right and ask yourself if nature planned this or something went wrong

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with cheeks that slowly rounded out on both sides over a couple of years.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And both sides of the lower jaw were ballooned out by matching bubbly cysts.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Cherubism

👀 What you see:
Both sides of the jaw are expanded by symmetrical bubbly cysts.

🦴 Why it matters:
A genetic condition replaces the jawbone with fibrous cystic tissue in childhood.

💊 Treatment:

Often it just needs watching because it tends to shrink on its own after puberty and surgery is saved for the severe cases.
